data.description:

KMD Brands Limited ( (NZ:KMD) ) just unveiled an update.

KMD Brands Limited has issued 351,832,889 fully paid ordinary shares at NZ$0.06 each as part of the retail component of its underwritten accelerated renounceable entitlement offer announced on 31 March 2026. The new shares, which rank equally with existing stock and represent about 24.3% of the company’s ordinary share capital, lift KMD’s total shares on issue to 1,799,415,022, significantly expanding its equity base and potentially diluting existing holdings while providing fresh capital to support its strategic and operational needs.

The most recent analyst rating on (NZ:KMD) stock is a Buy with a N$0.14 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on KMD Brands Limited stock, see the NZ:KMD Stock Forecast page.

More about KMD Brands Limited

KMD Brands Limited, listed on the NZX under ticker KMD, operates in the retail and apparel sector, best known for its outdoor and lifestyle brands. The company issues fully paid ordinary shares in both NZD and AUD, reflecting its presence across Australasian capital markets and its focus on broadening its investor base.

Hamelin Gold Limited ( (AU:HMG) ) has shared an announcement.

Hamelin Gold Limited has disclosed a change in the securities interests held by director Philip Crutchfield, as required under ASX listing rules. The notice details both his direct and indirect holdings, including interests held via private entities and a spouse, reflecting an updated record of his relevant interests in the company.

The update formalises the director’s revised position in the share register and reinforces the company’s compliance with continuous disclosure obligations. While the announcement does not indicate any change to Hamelin Gold’s operations or strategy, it provides transparency for investors regarding board-level equity exposure and governance oversight.

data.title: Odessa Minerals Expands Base Metal Targets at Lyndon Project
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Odessa Minerals Limited ( (AU:ODE) ).

Odessa Minerals has reported new base metal anomalism at its Lyndon Project in Western Australia, expanding the Ebro Bore mineralised strike to about 2.5 kilometres and reinforcing the scale potential of this emerging system. Additional sampling at the nearby Tower Bore prospect has identified anomalous base metals within a 6.6-kilometre dolomitic breccia associated with strong hyperspectral dolomite signals, providing a compelling new exploration target that closely parallels the mineralisation style at Ebro Bore.

These results strengthen Odessa’s pipeline of base metal opportunities at Lyndon and highlight the prospectivity of Devonian carbonates that host similar copper-lead-zinc deposits elsewhere in Western Australia. The company plans more systematic rock-chip sampling at Tower Bore to refine priority areas, a step that could materially advance its exploration strategy and enhance its positioning in the region’s base metals sector.

More about Odessa Minerals Limited

Odessa Minerals Limited is an Australian exploration company focused on base metals, particularly targeting copper, lead and zinc mineralisation. Its Lyndon Project in Western Australia is centred on Devonian carbonate host rocks, a geological setting known in the region for Mississippi Valley-type deposits, positioning the company within a prospective and historically productive mining corridor.

Average Trading Volume: 2,787,186

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: A$33.51M

Argo Investments Limited ( (AU:ARG) ) has issued an announcement.

Argo Investments is organising a series of investor information meetings across major Australian capital cities in May, covering Adelaide, Brisbane, Sydney, Canberra, Perth and Melbourne. The sessions will provide updates on the Argo Investments portfolio, its market outlook, and details on Argo Global Listed Infrastructure, including input from its specialist New York-based portfolio manager.

AoFrio Limited ( (NZ:AOF) ) has shared an announcement.

AoFrio Limited has called its 2026 Annual Meeting of shareholders for Wednesday, 27 May 2026 at 1:00 p.m., offering a hybrid format that allows investors to attend either in person at its Auckland headquarters or online via a dedicated virtual meeting platform. The agenda includes addresses from the chair and chief executive, shareholder questions, and an ordinary resolution authorising directors to set the auditor’s remuneration, with detailed instructions provided for voting, proxy appointments and postal ballots ahead of the meeting.

The company has set 22 May 2026 as the vote-eligibility date, and requires proxy and postal voting forms to be received by 1:00 p.m. on 25 May 2026 to be counted. By formalising hybrid attendance options and clarifying voting mechanics, AoFrio aims to maximise shareholder engagement and ensure orderly decision-making on audit fee-setting and any other business that may be lawfully raised at the meeting.

More about AoFrio Limited

AoFrio Limited is a New Zealand-based company listed on the local market, with its registered office in Rosedale, Auckland. The company engages with a broad shareholder base and uses hybrid physical and virtual meeting formats to support investor participation in its governance processes.

data.title: Nex Metals crystallises Arika JV value and advances Kookynie tailings push
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Nex Metals Explorations Ltd ( (AU:NME) ) has shared an announcement.

Nex Metals Explorations has agreed to divest its 20% interest in the Arika joint venture covering the Yundamindra and Kookynie Gold Projects to Arika Resources for $2.5 million in cash, largely offsetting more than $2 million in accrued JV contributions, plus 70.8 million Arika shares. The deal, timed to strong gold prices, crystallises value while preserving indirect exposure to the assets and is subject to shareholder approval at a May 2026 extraordinary general meeting.

The company advanced metallurgical test work at its wholly owned Kookynie Tailings Project, confirming existing NME and WTAC equipment suits a capital-light processing flowsheet and submitting bulk samples to ALS Metallurgy. Results will guide a drilling program scheduled to start in May 2026 and support broader strategic growth, including expanded Western Australian gold collaboration with WTAC and evaluation of entry into Egypt’s underexplored Eastern Desert.

More about Nex Metals Explorations Ltd

Nex Metals Explorations Ltd is an ASX-listed gold project explorer and developer focused on Western Australian goldfields and emerging opportunities in Egypt’s Eastern Desert. The company advances projects such as the Kookynie Tailings Project in partnership with the Wangkatja Tjungukla Aboriginal Corporation under profit-sharing and rehabilitation agreements.

Average Trading Volume: 141,293

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: A$6.04M

data.title: UC RUSAL Replaces Securities Registrar and Approves New Service Agreements
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

United Co RUSAL ( (HK:0486) ) has shared an announcement.

United Company RUSAL has disclosed that its board of directors approved a change of the company’s securities registrar following a duly convened meeting with a full quorum. The board voted to terminate its existing register maintenance agreement with Joint-Stock Company Interregional Registrar Center, resolving to end the 2020 contract covering the register of holders of the company’s securities.

The board simultaneously approved Limited Liability Company TEMION as the new registrar for UC RUSAL and endorsed the key terms for two new agreements. These agreements cover both the maintenance of the shareholder register and the organization of general meetings of shareholders, signalling an operational shift in the handling of shareholder records and corporate governance processes.

More about United Co RUSAL

United Company RUSAL is an international public joint-stock company incorporated in Jersey and continued in the Russian Federation, with shares listed in Hong Kong and on Russian exchanges. The company operates in the metals sector as one of the world’s major aluminium producers, serving industrial customers and investors across multiple markets.
